Interactive Visualizations

Author

Junwei Quan

1 Interactive Visualizations

This page provides interactive visualizations for the final project on University of Toronto machine-learning publications in OpenAlex. The figures are designed to complement the written report by allowing users to explore annual trends, publication-level citation patterns, and model interpretation results.

1.1 Annual publication output

Figure 1. This interactive line chart shows the annual number of keyword-defined machine-learning works affiliated with the University of Toronto from 2015 to 2025.

1.2 Open access and international collaboration

Figure 2. This interactive chart compares open-access share and international-collaboration share over time. Users can click the legend to show or hide either metric.

1.3 Citation explorer

Figure 3. This publication-level citation explorer shows the relationship between team size and citation impact. Hover text provides paper-level metadata including title, year, venue, topic, and citation count.

1.4 Model variable importance

Figure 4. This interactive bar chart shows permutation importance from the best-performing XGBoost model. Larger values indicate that shuffling the feature caused a greater decrease in predictive performance.
